basics.name: Jianwen Cao
basics.summary: M.Sc. Student in Informatics (AI) at University of Zurich (UZH). Interested in Computer Vision, Robotics, and Multimodal Models.

work.company: Computer Vision and Geometry Group (ETHZ CVG)
work.summary: Reproduced and improved GS-LIVO to achieve better geometric accuracy and mapping efficiency, reaching SOTA performance.
Conducted autoregressive pre-training on multimodal data, significantly improving the performance and robustness of downstream vision and control tasks.
Developed object detection algorithms based on improved DETR series architectures.

education.area: Informatics (Major: Artificial Intelligence, Minor: Data Science)
courses: Machine Learning for Natural Language Processing

education.institution: Nanjing University of Information Science and Technology (NUIST)

publications.name: Generative Event Pretraining with Foundation Model Alignment
publications.summary: Proposed a multimodal pre-training method combining images and event cameras, achieving SOTA performance.

publications.name: Memory Over Maps: 3D Object Localization Without Reconstruction
publications.summary: Responsible for 3D object localization (query input, 3D position output) and efficiency analysis. Ultimately deployed on the Boston Dynamics Spot robot dog.

publications.name: Fuse Tune: Hierarchical Decoder Towards Efficient Transfer Learning
publications.summary: Proposed an efficient transfer learning method that eliminates the need for backbone network backpropagation.

portfolio.name: Real-time Visual-Inertial-LiDAR Gaussian Splatting SLAM
portfolio.description: Reproduced and improved GS-LIVO to achieve better geometric accuracy and mapping efficiency, reaching SOTA performance.

portfolio.name: Event Camera-Enhanced Robot Motor Control Policies
portfolio.description: Conducted autoregressive pre-training on multimodal data, significantly improving the performance and robustness of downstream vision and control tasks.
